mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and consumption contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contractions in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based demand destruction across consumer and residential investment sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of falling RSAFS and falling HOUST fails to produce negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During elevated inflation regimes, month-over-month deceleration in consumer price inflation causes the market to reduce expected terminal policy rates, lowering discount rates and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL decelerates while the index level exceeds 300 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals acute manufacturing sector stress that spills into aggregate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of instances when the trigger fires over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling nonfarm payrolls concurrent with a rising federal funds rate produce a policy-induced growth scare where tighter monetary policy meets weakening labor income, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining PAYEMS and rising FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payrolls signals persistent labor market tightness that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain higher interest rates for longer, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of months when PAYEMS accelerates while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in industrial production and a rising unemployment rate confirm a synchronized downturn in supply and labor markets that erodes corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to produce negative 5-day returns in greater than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.
